title: Automate Earliest Available Time Slot Allocation with Gap
description: The system shall automatically allocate the earliest available 5-minute time slot that includes a mandatory 3-minute gap before and after any existing allocations.
rationale: To ensure safe separation between flights by providing appropriate time intervals between them.
business_value: Enhances flight safety and operational efficiency by reducing manual errors in scheduling.

source.description: As a air traffic controller I need the system to automatically allocate the earliest available 5-minute time slot with a mandatory 3-minute gap between consecutive slots So that safe separation between flights is ensured.

acceptance_criteria: The system assigns the earliest 5-minute slot that respects a 3-minute gap before and after any existing allocations.
preconditions: A schedule with existing allocated slots is available.
Current time or a given reference time is known.
postconditions: A new 5-minute time slot has been allocated with the mandatory 3-minute gap enforced.
functional_constraints: The allocation algorithm must consider both the start and end times of existing slots.
The system must accurately account for the 3-minute gap in its calculations.

non_functional_requirements.performance: The time slot allocation process should complete within a reasonable time frame to avoid delays in air traffic operations.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available at all times to ensure continuous operation of the air traffic control center.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should be able to handle an increasing number of flight schedules without significant performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error handling mechanisms should provide clear and actionable feedback to the user in case of allocation failures.

edge_cases: What happens if there are no available slots that meet the criteria?
How does the system handle time zone differences between different air traffic control centers?
What happens when a new slot overlaps with an existing slot despite the gap?
dependencies: Time reference for calculation (current or given)

interfaces.name: Flight Schedule Management API
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with this API to retrieve and update the existing flight schedules.

interfaces.name: Air Traffic Control Console
interfaces.description: The requirement interfaces with this UI to receive user requests for slot allocations and display the results.

test_cases.description: Request allocation when no existing slots are available.
test_cases.expected_result: System returns an error message indicating no available slots.

test_cases.description: Request allocation with a time zone difference.
test_cases.expected_result: System correctly calculates and allocates the earliest slot respecting the 3-minute gap, considering the time zone differences.

test_cases.description: Request allocation when new slots overlap despite the gap.
test_cases.expected_result: System handles the overlap by finding another available slot that adheres to the mandatory 3-minute gap.
